# Test fixtures

Small, synthetic, de-identified recordings the backend test suite reads as
input. **No real clinical data.** All signals are deterministic sine waves plus
seeded noise (RNG seed `42`), 2 seconds long, with a fixed synthetic
acquisition date (2020-01-01).

## Provenance

Every file here is generated by the reproducible fixture generator. To
regenerate byte-for-byte (subject to the export backends' own output
stability):

```
uv sync --frozen
uv run python tools/make_dev_data.py
```

The generator (`tools/make_dev_data.py`) writes these committed fixtures via
`write_test_fixtures()`, in addition to the gitignored `dev-data/` debugging
tree. They are committed rather than generated at test time so a fixture only
changes when someone deliberately regenerates it: an incidental `mne`/`edfio`/
`eeglabio` version bump cannot silently alter the bytes under test.

## Files

| File | Format | Contents |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `eeg_continuous.edf` | continuous EDF | 8 scalp-EEG channels, 256 Hz |
| `ieeg_continuous.edf` | continuous EDF | 6 sEEG channels, 512 Hz |
| `eeg_embedded.set` | EEGLAB SET | data embedded in the `.set`, no companion |
| `eeg_split.set` + `eeg_split.fdt` | EEGLAB SET/FDT | metadata `.set` referencing a separate `.fdt` data file |
| `eeg_epoched.set` | EEGLAB SET | epoched (`trials > 1`); drives the continuous-only rejection path |
| `eeg_metadata.json` | JSON | representative EEG sidecar parameters |
| `ieeg_metadata.json` | JSON | representative iEEG sidecar parameters |

`eeg_split.fdt` is the data companion for `eeg_split.set` and is only valid
alongside it; removing it drives the missing-companion error path.
